title: toxicodendron coriaria
intro: Toxicodendron coriaria, also known as poison sumac, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family and is native to the southeastern United States and parts of Asia. The plant contains urushiol, a highly allergenic oil that causes severe skin irritation and is responsible for its toxic properties. Despite its toxicity, poison sumac has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Poison sumac is typically prepared as a tincture or tea for medicinal use, but caution should be exercised when handling the plant due to its highly allergenic nature.
